evidence_quotes.quote: they should not need to worry about extra fees if they decide to cancel
I want my child to be able to use entertainment such as video games without worry that they will become addicted to the "gambling" mechanics, that is often hidden behind "in-game currency".

paragraphs.text: It's quite often that such initiatives go unnoticed for many. The practices listed in bullet points is everything that I would support. Only by voicing our opinion and constantly laying down the right foundation we can establish better future not only for ourselves, also for our future generations. If we will not have the right regulations in place, we will only go downhill so you have my full support. Although I am fairly knowledgeable to different types of exploits used by corporations due to personal experience, I am writing this to support people who are not. I want my child to be able to use services freely without worry of being exploited, should they decide to subscribe to a service, they should not need to worry about extra fees if they decide to cancel. We should not be hold hostage due to the demands of companies. I want my child to be able to use entertainment such as video games without worry that they will become addicted to the "gambling" mechanics, that is often hidden behind "in-game currency". I want the companies to be held responsible with substantial fines applied to them should they break laws and regulations, otherwise there is no incentive to change. Looking at Google, Microsoft, Facebook as an example, many small fines yet they still break laws because its a low impact compared to rewards they get from illegal activity. The fines should be fair, but substantial so it has an effect. Finally, I would highly recommend checking 'Stop Destroying Games' initiative, which falls into similar bucket in protecting consumer rights.
